mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and signals easier financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is falling.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative or below unconditional baseline in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month lookback, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% tightens credit and compresses valuation multiples, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed upward.
failure_condition: Reject if upward crossings above 4.5% are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the median post-trigger return is not below the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates deteriorating labor income and weaker household spending, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when unemployment is moving higher.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of episodes where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, or if PAYEMS does not fall concurrently in the same episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is flat or declining while HOUST remains weak, it signals demand slowdown in retail and housing at the same time, and that joint weakness should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term adds no out-of-sample predictive value versus each series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative when both RSAFS growth and HOUST are below their 6-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: A stronger INDPRO reading after a prior contraction indicates stabilization in industrial output and inventories, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as recession fears ease.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average after a decline
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of episodes where INDPRO turns back above its 3-month moving average after a prior 2-month decline.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand pulse from rising GDPC1 and RSAFS should support corporate revenues and boost S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days, especially when both series are accelerating together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not improve 60-day forward returns relative to either series alone, or if the interaction signal has negative Rank IC over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is falling and UNRATE is rising together, labor market deterioration is broad rather than temporary, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE pattern fails to outperform each proxy separately, or if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime, a falling CPIAUCSL reading alongside unchanged or lower FEDFUNDS indicates real-rate relief and reduced policy pressure, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS signal does not yield positive 60-day returns more often than not during disinflation, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL trend is flat or rising.
